title: Keynu Knowledge Graph Engine and Mission Manager
goal: Build automatic project-context restoration, architecture graph visualization, and live runtime workflow tracing for Keynu and connected applications.
completedMilestones: Initial project scanner and graph snapshot store
Mission Manager architecture and type definitions
Mission registry loader and state store
Mission Bootstrap builder and BrowserAgent integration
rules: Use PowerShell only when Node.js cannot perform the operation.
Use small independently verifiable KAP jobs.
Read back every created or modified file.
Run TypeScript build after code integration changes.
Preserve the active Keynu browser connection.
Do not overwrite unrelated working-tree changes.

architectureDecisions.title: Repository memory is the source of truth
architectureDecisions.summary: Project knowledge must live in repository-managed mission and memory files rather than depending on one AI chat.

architectureDecisions.title: KAP is an application-level protocol
architectureDecisions.summary: KAP defines Keynu mission, job, report, error, evidence, and control semantics while transport may use browser chat, files, HTTP, WebSocket, JSON-RPC, or MCP.

architectureDecisions.title: Node.js-first runtime architecture
architectureDecisions.summary: Use native Node.js APIs for Runtime operations and use PowerShell only when Node.js cannot perform the required operation.

architectureDecisions.title: Verified execution evidence
architectureDecisions.summary: An AI must not claim local completion until a matching KAP REPORT provides real execution evidence and verification.

recommendedReading.title: KAP 1.0 specification
recommendedReading.reason: Required to understand KAP envelopes, message flow, and AI response rules.

recommendedReading.title: KAP architecture requirements
recommendedReading.reason: Defines reliability, correlation, retry, schemas, chunking, and security work still required.

recommendedReading.title: Keynu Agent Protocol book chapter
recommendedReading.reason: Explains how KAP fits into the wider Keynu architecture.

recommendedReading.title: Development protocol
recommendedReading.reason: Defines mandatory rules for AI-assisted Keynu development.

recommendedReading.title: Repository memory ADR
recommendedReading.reason: Explains why repository memory replaces dependence on chat history.

knownLimitations: Mission and memory files require explicit updates when milestones are completed; automatic milestone completion is not implemented yet.
A full recovery test with a completely new chat or unfamiliar AI has not yet been completed.
The current memory documents contain some older Dashboard priorities and require synchronization with the active mission.

nextActions.title: Run a clean new-chat recovery test
nextActions.reason: Connect Keynu to a completely new conversation and verify that an unfamiliar AI understands KAP, returns MISSION_ACK, restores the active mission, and selects the correct next action without manual project explanation.

nextActions.title: Record recovery-test outcome
nextActions.reason: Persist success or failure evidence in the repository-backed mission definition.

nextActions.title: Continue with Mission Dashboard
nextActions.reason: Begin the next product milestone only after independent chat recovery has been proven.
